display.purpose: Hero headlines on marketing surfaces and top-of-empty-state moments. Tight tracking; the type does the work.

headline.purpose: Page titles in app screens — the 'you are here' type.

title.purpose: Card titles, section headers, dialog headings.

body.purpose: Default text. Tight enough for dense dashboards, comfortable for paragraphs.

body-small.purpose: Table cells, dense lists, secondary descriptions.

label.purpose: Form labels, axis labels, badge text. Slightly tighter, slightly tracked.

mono.purpose: Numeric values, IDs, code, citation URLs — wherever character alignment matters.

shadows.name: shadow-2xs
shadows.purpose: Hairline lift on inputs and ghost surfaces — barely perceptible.

shadows.name: shadow-xl
shadows.purpose: Reserved for full-screen modals and major overlays.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Default easing for reveal, card-lift, and state transitions. Used on the .reveal and .card-lift utilities.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Generic state transitions where ease-out-expo is too dramatic.

motion.name: duration-base
motion.purpose: Card lift, popover open, drawer slide.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most important action on a surface. One per screen.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; background: #c91f1f; color: #ffffff; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #b81c1c; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #f4f5f7, 0 0 0 4px #c91f1f; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.5; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Outline Button
components.description: The default 'do something' button when there isn't a single primary.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; background: transparent; color: #141a23; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: 1px solid #e3e5ea; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #eff0f3; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #f4f5f7, 0 0 0 4px #c91f1f; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Tertiary actions inside dense surfaces — table row actions, toolbar buttons.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 40px; padding: 0 16px; background: transparent; color: #141a23; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #eff0f3; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #f4f5f7, 0 0 0 4px #c91f1f; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default form field. Workspace-fill 'well' rather than a raised piece.
components.css: .ds-input { display: block; width: 280px; height: 40px; padding: 0 12px; background: #f4f5f7; color: #141a23; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; border: 1px solid #e3e5ea; border-radius: 6px; transition: box-shadow 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6b7280; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #f4f5f7, 0 0 0 4px #c91f1f; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Default elevated surface for grouped content. Flat at rest.
components.html:
AI visibility
Where your brand appears across AI answer surfaces.
42citations this week
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 16px; width: 320px; padding: 24px; background: #ffffff; color: #141a23; border: 1px solid #e3e5ea; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0px 1px 3px 0px hsl(220 20% 20% / 0.06); font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-card-title { font-size: 20px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.2; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-card-desc { font-size: 13px; color: #6b7280; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-card-metric { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 8px; padding-top: 8px; border-top: 1px solid #e3e5ea; } .ds-card-num { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', Monaco, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: #141a23; } .ds-card-unit { font-size: 13px; color: #6b7280; }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Default state pill — Surface Mute fill, foreground text. Brand red is reserved.
components.html: Indexed
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; padding: 2px 10px; background: #eff0f3; color: #141a23; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; letter-spacing: 0.02em; border-radius: 9999px; }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: Persistent app navigation rail item. Active state uses the dark slate, not the brand accent.
components.html: CitationsGEO RankingsArticles
components.css: .ds-sidebar { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; width: 240px; padding: 12px; background: #ffffff; border-right: 1px solid #e3e5ea; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; padding: 8px 12px; color: #6b7280; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 6px; transition: background 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1), color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #eff0f3; color: #141a23; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: #191e29; color: #ffffff; } .ds-nav-item-active:hover { background: #191e29; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: KPI Tile
components.description: Signature dashboard primitive: number leads, label and delta follow. Tabular figures, mono for the number.
components.html:
AI Citations — last 7 days
1,284
+12.4% vs prior period
components.css: .ds-kpi { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; width: 240px; padding: 20px 24px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e3e5ea; border-radius: 8px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-kpi-label { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #6b7280; text-transform: none; } .ds-kpi-value { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', Monaco, Consolas, monospace; font-size: 32px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.1; color: #141a23; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.01em; } .ds-kpi-delta { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; color: #36a169; }

narrative.overview:
VentureCite is an instrument, not a brochure. It exists to give marketing operators a calm, dense, trustworthy view of how brands appear inside AI answer surfaces — and what to do about it. The visual system reflects that: a near-monochrome workspace built on cool-tinted neutrals, an editorial sans-serif, restrained shadows, and one disciplined accent — a strong vermillion-leaning red — that earns its presence by appearing rarely and only on what matters most.

The system explicitly rejects the four reflex aesthetics of contemporary SaaS: the AI-tool cliché, the old-school SEO chrome, the generic SaaS template, and crypto/web3 maximalism. The look should sit alongside otterly.ai and peec.ai — clean, confident, data-forward — and feel like Linear's discipline meeting Stripe Press's editorial calm.

Density varies by surface: long-form pages breathe with editorial rhythm; operational dashboards tighten up for fast scanning. The accent is never decoration. Numbers come before conclusions. Every pixel reports.
keyCharacteristics: Cool-tinted off-white workspace; pure-white cards; type at near-black
One saturated accent (vermillion red), used on ≤10% of any screen
Inter for everything except numeric/code (JetBrains Mono); no serif in chrome
Soft, low-contrast shadows for ambient lift, not drama
Moderate rounding (6–8px) — neither pill-soft nor brutalist-sharp
Light theme is canonical; dark theme is a peer, not a marketing aesthetic

rules.name: The Earned-Accent Rule
rules.body: The vermillion red appears on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point. If you find yourself reaching for it twice on the same surface, one of those uses is wrong.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: #000 and #fff exist only on the card surface and inside icons. All text, borders, and backgrounds are tinted toward the cool neutral hue. Pure black is for ink-on-paper; we are on a screen.

rules.name: The Status-Never-Brand Rule
rules.body: Destructive red and brand red are different values for a reason. Errors must never share the brand accent — confusing semantics for identity erodes both.

rules.name: The Sans-Only-In-Chrome Rule
rules.body: Inter handles every chrome surface. Long-form content (glossary entries, blog/report bodies) is the only place a serif may legitimately appear, and only inside the prose container.

rules.name: The 65–75ch Rule
rules.body: Body copy in long-form surfaces caps at 65–75 characters per line. Wider lines fail to read at scan speed; this is the editorial discipline, not a suggestion.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numerals Rule
rules.body: Anywhere numbers stack vertically — tables, charts, ranking lists, KPIs — use mono or the tabular-nums feature. Proportional digits in a numeric column is a tell of carelessness.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: Operational surfaces (dashboard cards, list rows, sidebar items) sit flat at rest. Shadow appears only as a state change — hover, focus, drag, or genuine elevation. Ambient shadow on every card is the SaaS template tell; we don't do that.

rules.name: The No-Glass Rule
rules.body: Glassmorphism is forbidden. backdrop-filter: blur may appear on full-viewport overlays (dialogs over content) and nowhere else. Translucent panels stacked on translucent panels is not depth — it's confusion.

dos: Do lead with the data. Numbers, ranks, and signals appear before any verdict copy — interpretation follows evidence.
Do use the brand red sparingly and only on what matters most: the primary CTA, the focus ring, the active rank indicator, the chart-1 series.
Do keep cards flat at rest. Shadows respond to state, not as ambient decoration.
Do pair every status color with an icon, label, or shape. Color is never the sole encoding.
Do use tabular-nums (or JetBrains Mono) anywhere numeric data stacks vertically.
Do keep long-form body copy inside a 65–75ch container with editorial spacing.
Do use the cool-tinted off-white (#f4f5f7) as the workspace and reserve pure white for elevated surfaces.
Do use Inter for all chrome; mono only for numeric/code.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion everywhere — disable reveal, float, pulse-soft, marquee, and gradient-shift animations under that media query.
Do use the dark-slate active state (#191e29) for sidebar/nav active items in light mode — anchor with weight, not the brand accent.
donts: Don't use gradient text. The text-gradient-red utility currently in client/src/index.css is a violation of this rule and should be retired; replace with solid text-primary plus weight or scale for emphasis.
Don't use purple-to-pink gradients, neon glow, glassmorphism, or sparkle/✨ iconography anywhere.
Don't ship a navy enterprise-blue dashboard, dense table-of-tables layouts, or every-metric-on-screen overload.
Don't lay out a marketing surface as a gradient hero + three identical feature cards + stock illustration trio.
Don't use saturated color decoratively. If a color isn't reporting a state or anchoring a primary action, it's wrong.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't use pure #000 or #fff for text or workspace backgrounds. Tint toward the cool neutral hue.
Don't wrap everything in a card. Most lists, headers, and forms don't need a container — and nested cards are always wrong.
Don't reach for a modal as the first thought. Exhaust inline/progressive alternatives first.
Don't use em dashes or -- in product copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't animate CSS layout properties. Animate transform and opacity only.
Don't mix the destructive red and the brand red on the same surface — semantic and identity must stay distinct.
Don't add shadow-lg or shadow-xl to a resting card. Those shadows are reserved for genuine overlay surfaces.
Don't use the brand red as a fill on chips, callouts, or status pills as a default.
